data.voice_note: Calm, warm, pragmatic, quietly witty. Sentence case. Health-framed, never convenience-fee framed. Every nutrition mention is an estimate. No medical claims. One-tap always honoured — never imply silent auto-ordering.

ads.headline: Stop deciding lunch. Start tapping yes.
ads.primary_text: You already order in every day. Rasa plans your month around your macros and your budget, then nudges you at mealtime: 'Grilled chicken bowl, ₹240, arrives ~12:35 — confirm?' You tap yes. That's the whole thing. No 4pm 'what do I eat.'

ads.headline: Hit your protein target without cooking or tracking.
ads.primary_text: Your meal prep dies by Wednesday. Rasa orders restaurant food that fits your macros — estimated across the day, honest about it — so you stay on plan while ordering in. Delivery apps sell convenience; trackers tell you what you ate. Rasa actually orders food that fits your numbers.

ads.headline: Eat out daily. Still end the month under your number.
ads.primary_text: That ₹8-15k delivery bill you can't account for? Rasa sets a hard monthly cap, spreads it across the month, applies coupons, and keeps a ledger you can actually see. Order in every day — and stay under the cap you set. Never a fee on your food.

ads.headline: An AI meal planner that never spends your money silently.
ads.primary_text: Skeptical of 'AI orders your food'? So were we. Rasa plans your month, but every single order needs your one-tap confirm — dish, price, arrival time, then you tap. No tap, no charge. Allergens hard-blocked before anything hits your cart. Macros labelled as estimates. It's the boring, honest version — which is the version you'd actually trust.

ads.headline: A month of meals, one tap each | Rasa
ads.primary_text: Planned to your macros and your budget, ordered via Swiggy a tap at a time. No daily decision, no blown budget. Health-framed subscription from ₹149/mo.

ads.headline: Eat like an adult who has their life handled.
ads.primary_text: Not a diet coach yelling macros. The competent friend who cooks. Rasa quietly plans your month, keeps you roughly on your numbers, holds your budget — and hands you a 5-second tap at mealtimes. Sorted.

ads.headline: Convenience gets you in. Macros and budget are why you stay.
ads.primary_text: Card 1: No more 4pm 'what do I eat.' Card 2: Food that fits your protein target, estimated across the day. Card 3: A hard monthly cap you actually hold. Card 4: One tap per order — nothing spent silently. Rasa: taste, balanced.

ads.headline: Your Swiggy history called. It wants an intervention.
ads.primary_text: Same three restaurants, peak-hour prices, protein nowhere in sight. Rasa plans your month to your macros and your cap, and orders a tap at a time. Kinder to your body and your bill — and still you, tapping yes.
